claim: WHO estimates over 300000 babies born with SCD annually, 75 percent in Sub-Saharan Africa, with 50-90 percent mortality before age five without newborn screening and penicillin prophylaxis.

source.title: WHO Sickle cell disease fact sheet

method: Reviewed WHO AFRO SCD fact sheet and disease-burden estimates.
limitations: Birth-prevalence estimates are modeled, not directly measured in all areas.
Mortality estimates depend on care access, which varies substantially.

claim: A 2017 PLOS Medicine study mapped sickle cell allele frequency globally at 5km resolution, estimating 305800 SCD births annually with Nigeria, DRC, and India having the highest absolute numbers.

source.title: Global epidemiology of sickle haemoglobin in neonates

method: Reviewed mapping methodology, allele-frequency estimation, and birth-prevalence projections.
limitations: 5km estimates are modeled and may not reflect local variation.
Maps reflect allele frequency, not diagnosed disease prevalence.
